task.title: Implement Deformable Component Assembly benchmarking scenario with industrial dataset integration in KubeEdge-Ianvs
task.description: KubeEdge-Ianvs (issue #197) requires a complete industrial embodied intelligence benchmarking scenario for Deformable Component Assembly — specifically the precision assembly of thin and soft components such as FPC flexible circuit boards onto mobile phone motherboards. This task requires implementing the full benchmark scenario from scratch within the Ianvs framework. The agent must: (1) understand the existing Ianvs benchmark architecture by studying examples/llm_simple_qa/ and the distributed synergy AI benchmarking specification to learn how scenarios, datasets, and test environments are structured, (2) implement the dataset integration layer that loads a Kaggle-hosted industrial assembly dataset into the Ianvs unified format — understanding how dataset loaders work in core/testcasecontroller/ and how ground truth is defined for manipulation tasks, (3) implement the scenario configuration YAML and Python wiring following the pattern in existing examples, (4) add structured loggers at key benchmark stages (data loading, inference, evaluation) using the Ianvs logging convention in core/common/, (5) fix all relative import paths that break when the benchmark is run from different working directories, (6) write a comprehensive README following Ianvs documentation standards, and (7) ensure the baseline algorithm runs end-to-end and produces quantifiable performance metrics. The core challenge is that Ianvs has a deeply layered plugin architecture: scenario → testenv → algorithm → dataset → metrics. The agent must understand all five layers and how they compose before making any change.

ground_truth.acceptance_criteria: Benchmark runs end-to-end without import errors or path errors. Dataset loads successfully from the configured source. Baseline algorithm produces accuracy and throughput metrics. Logger outputs structured INFO/DEBUG messages at each stage. README contains setup, run, and results interpretation sections.

metadata.notes: This is Ronak's own LFX Mentee project (mentors: Zimu Zheng, Shijing Hu). PR #292 implements the Deformable Component Assembly benchmark from issue #197 (Embodied Intelligence Benchmarking for Industrial Manufacturing). The dataset is hosted on Kaggle. This task is uniquely powerful for the benchmark because it tests the agent's ability to understand a multi-layer plugin architecture (scenario→testenv→algorithm→dataset→metrics) — identical in complexity to what the GSoC eval dataset project itself is building.
